title: Design launch-readiness deliverables: product demo video + social assets

Based on approved brand direction (ALT-1357: The Electric Lab archetype, dark-mode-first indigo, teal=AI/orange=human semantics).

title: Provide visual direction and assets for ALT-1483 dashboard AI search bar + workflow copilot
description:

Needed deliverables:

- Motion and interaction specs for loading, empty, hover, focus, submit, and streaming states

Implementation constraints to design around:
- Existing frontend is React + Vite + Tailwind
- New UI must fit current dashboard/workflow builder structure without heavy runtime overhead
- Copilot responses may stream, so long-running interaction states need to be specified cleanly

title: Brand asset scaffolding: initial artifacts, MemPalace entries, asset review
description:

   - Marketing templates (stubs)

2. **MemPalace brand/* entries** — for every artifact published, create a MemPalace entry with:
   - `{ git_path, cdn_url, version, owner, approved_at, consumers }`
   - Knowledge graph edges: integrations → logos, skills → tiles, surfaces → direction docs

3. **Asset review** — review CTO's audit inventory (from [ALT-1361](/ALT/issues/ALT-1361)) and decide what to keep, redesig

title: Design Show HN launch social graphics (Twitter/X + LinkedIn) — April 7
description:
## Context

AutoFlow is submitting a Show HN post on **Tuesday April 7 at 8:00 AM ET**. Immediately after submission, we run a social amplification blast on Twitter/X and LinkedIn.

## Deliverables

Create graphics optimized for each platform:

### Twitter/X
- 1 announcement graphic (1200×675px) — AutoFlow logo + headline: "We just launched on Hacker News — check us out!"
- Include HN orange color (#FF6600) as accent
- Clean, developer-friendly aesthetic

### LinkedIn
- 1 announcement graphic (1200×627px) — professional variant of the same

## Brand Notes
- Product: AutoFlow — AI workflow automation platform
- Tagline: "AI workflow automation built on composable templates"
- URL: autoflow.app
- Tone: developer-friendly, clean, confident

## Deadline

**Monday April 6 by end of day** — graphics need to be ready before the Tuesday launch.

## Output

Post final assets as attachments on this task, or share links for CMO review.

title: Create 3 featured blog images — due Apr 6 EOD
description:
## URGENT — Launch Blocker

Week 1 blog posts publish **Mon Apr 7 at 9am EST**. Featured images are needed by **Apr 6 EOD** or the launch slips.

## Images Required (all 1200x630px)

1. "Complete Guide to Workflow Automation for Small Business"
2. "Automate Invoice Follow-ups in 5 Minutes"
3. "Future of No-Code Automation"

## Design Brief

Full concepts, color palettes, and specs are in [ALT-437](/ALT/issues/ALT-437).

Brand: AutoFlow — modern, clean, SaaS aesthetic. Use helloautoflow.com brand colors.

## Deliverables

- 3x JPG or PNG at 1200x630px
- Upload to this issue as attachments
- Confirm completion via comment here by Apr 6 EOD

## Why critical

Content Lead has all 3 posts written and SEO-optimized. Design assets are the only remaining blocker for Monday launch.

You will use Vercel for wire framing the visuals for the AutoFlow application\service being launched on Azure.

title: Design spec: Observability dashboard live feed and KPI prototype
description:
## Context
Frontend implementation for [ALT-1939](/ALT/issues/ALT-1939) is blocked by required design direction.

## Request
Provide the visual and interaction spec for the Sprint 1 observability dashboard surface covering:
- live activity feed shell
- one KPI card prototype
- time-range and filter controls
- SSE status / fallback-to-polling states

## Required Deliverables
1. Color, typography, spacing, and layout direction for the new dashboard surface
2. Motion and interaction-state specs for loading, live, reconnecting, degraded/polling, empty, and error states
3. Any icons or visual assets needed for transport/status indicators
4. Explicit sign-off that the direction is approved for implementation and merge

## Frontend Constraints
- Existing app stack is React + Vite + Tailwind in `dashboard/`
- Surface must preserve room for more cards/charts in Sprint 2
- Need responsive behavior for desktop and smaller laptop widths
- Avoid heavy animation or bespoke asset work that will slow dashboard rendering

## Done
Comment on [ALT-1939](/ALT/issues/ALT-1939) with approved specs and sign-off so frontend implementation can begin.

Context:

- Frontend implementation is blo

title: Review and approve AutoFlow login-page rebrand for ALT-1647
description:
## Why
[ALT-1647](/ALT/issues/ALT-1647) implements a narrow rebrand on the existing dashboard login page to remove Microsoft visual references while keeping the current MSAL redirect flow intact.

## What needs design input
- Confirm the existing AutoFlow login surface can ship with the current layout and spacing
- Approve the CTA copy swap to `Sign in` and `Create your account`
- Confirm the removal of the Microsoft icon without a replacement mark
- Approve the use of existing `brand-600` / `brand-700` tokens for the primary and secondary buttons
- Confirm interaction/loading states are acceptable as implemented

## Current implementation status
Frontend has already prepared the local code changes on the checked-out worktree for:
- `dashboard/src/pages/Login.tsx`
- `dashboard/src/pages/Login.test.tsx`
- auth-related Playwright specs under `dashboard/e2e/`

Please comment on [ALT-1647](/ALT/issues/ALT-1647) with approval or requested visual adjustments so the frontend task can resume and be prepared for merge.

title: Design helloautoflow.com waitlist/launch landing page
description:
## Context

AutoFlow needs a high-converting landing page at helloautoflow.com for the May 5 launch. The page must drive waitlist signups and capture early leads.

## Deliverables

1. **Full page wireframe** — Desktop + mobile layouts
2. **High-fidelity mockup** — Production-ready design using the AutoFlow brand identity (from [ALT-654](/ALT/issues/ALT-654))
3. **CTA design** — Waitlist signup form placement and call-to-action copy (work with Content Lead for copy)
4. **Hero section** — Headline, subhead, product screenshot or animation placeholder
5. **Feature blocks** — 3-4 key feature highlights
6. **Social proof section** — Testimonial or beta user placeholder

## Requirements

- Above-the-fold signup form
- Mobile-first responsive
- Fast-loading (minimal animations)
- Brand-consistent (use [ALT-654](/ALT/issues/ALT-654) brand system once available)

## Deadline

**April 28** — design must be ready for handoff to engineering by April 28. Share draft with CMO by April 22.

## Dependencies

- [ALT-654](/ALT/issues/ALT-654) brand identity (due April 25 — start wireframes in parallel, apply brand when ready)

## Output

Deliver mockups as attachments. Post a design document summary

title: Create AutoFlow brand visual identity system and guidelines
description:
## Context

AutoFlow needs a complete visual identity system to ensure brand consistency across all marketing materials, the landing page, social media, and launch assets.

## Deliverables

1. **Logo** — Primary + horizontal lockup variants (SVG + PNG)
2. **Color palette** — Primary, secondary, and neutral colors with hex/RGB values
3. **Typography** — Font selections for headlines, body, UI elements
4. **Visual style guide** — One-page brand guidelines document (PDF or Notion page)
5. **Icon/symbol set** — Key product concept icons (workflow, templates, automation, AI)

## Brand Direction

- Product: AutoFlow — AI workflow automation platform
- Tagline: "AI workflow automation built on composable templates"
- Audience: developers, technical teams, startup operators
- Tone: clean, confident, technical but approachable — think Linear, Vercel aesthetic
- URL: autoflow.app / helloautoflow.com

## Deadline

**April 25** — needed before May 5 full launch. Share progress with CMO by April 18 for feedback.

## Output

Deliver all assets as attachments on this task. Post brand guidelines as a document.

title: Design mass email campaign signature block for helloautoflow.com
description:
## Objective

Create an attractive, deliverability-safe HTML email signature block for use in mass email campaigns using the **helloautoflow.com** domain.

## Requirements

### Visual
- Clean, professional design using AutoFlow brand colors and typography
- Include logo (text-based or small lightweight image)
- Name, title (or generic "AutoFlow Team"), and email @helloautoflow.com
- Link to `helloautoflow.com`
- Optional: LinkedIn or CTA button (keep it minimal)

### Email Deliverability (Critical)
- Prefer mostly-text with minimal images — heavy image-based signatures hurt deliverability
- If images are used: host externally with reliable CDN, use `alt` text, keep file size <10KB
- No tracking pixels in the signature itself
- Avoid large tables, excessive inline CSS, or broken HTML
- Test against spam filters (SpamAssassin guidelines)
- Provide both **HTML version** and **plain-text fallback**

### Deliverables
1. HTML signature block (copy-pasteable)
2. Plain-text version
3. Brief usage notes for SDRs on Gmail / Outlook setup

## Stakeholders
- **Graphic Designer**: owns visual design and HTML output
- **SDR1 + SDR2**: will use this in outbound campaigns — review for fit and usab

title: Dark mode visual signoff for dashboard pages
description: Provide visual-direction signoff and interaction-state validation for ALT-1344 dark mode parity across all top-level dashboard pages.

title: Design node-canvas workflow builder visual system (on-brand, not n8n clone)
description:
## Node-Canvas Workflow Builder — Visual Design System

Parent: [ALT-1275](/ALT/issues/ALT-1275)

### Context
CEO directed us to build a **node-based canvas workflow builder** (like n8n, Make, Zapier) instead of polishing the existing vertical step list. Critical requirement: the design must be **on-brand for AutoFlow**, not a copy of n8n.

### What We Need

1. **Node design** — Shape, size, border radius, shadow, icon placement for workflow step nodes. Must feel uniquely AutoFlow.
2. **Connector/edge styles** — Line type (bezier, straight, step), color, animation for data flow.
3. **Canvas grid** — Background pattern, snap grid, zoom controls.
4. **Color palette** — Node category colors (triggers, actions, conditions, integrations). Must work in both light and dark mode.
5. **Typography** — Font choices for node labels, descriptions, and canvas chrome.
6. **Interaction states** — Selected, hover, dragging, error, running, success.
7. **Toolbar/sidebar** — Node palette, search, properties panel layout.

### References (inspiration, not imitation)
- n8n: Dark navy canvas, node-based flow, orange accent
- Make.com: Colorful circular nodes, clean connections
- Zapier: Linear but visua

title: Design onboarding flow and first-run experience
description:
## Objective

Design the complete new-user onboarding flow optimized for first workflow live in <10 minutes (per [ALT-338](/ALT/issues/ALT-338) strategy).

## Screens Required

1. **Signup/welcome** — minimal friction, social login options, "no credit card required" badge
2. **Segment selection** — "For Teams" / "For Individuals" toggle (informs template recommendations)
3. **Use case picker** — "What do you want to automate?" with top categories (invoicing, lead routing, notifications, data sync, custom)
4. **First workflow wizard** — guided 3-step setup with the default "daily digest" template pre-loaded
5. **Celebration screen** — dynamic time/effort saved estimate after first workflow runs (e.g., "You just saved ~45 minutes this week")
6. **14-day Pro trial banner** — persistent but non-intrusive indicator of trial status

## Design Principles

- Minimize steps to value — every screen must move the user closer to their first running workflow
- Progressive disclosure — don't overwhelm with features upfront
- Social proof at key moments — "12,000 teams on Pro" or beta user quotes
- Clear escape hatches — skip buttons that don't feel punishing

## Deliverables

- Full-fidelity moc

title: Design Product Hunt gallery assets and email header graphics
description:
## Objective

Create the visual assets needed for Product Hunt launch and email campaigns.

## Product Hunt Gallery (Priority 1)

Design 5-6 gallery images that showcase AutoFlow. These must be polished and conversion-optimized — PH gallery is the #1 factor in click-through.

**Required slides:**
1. Hero shot — AutoFlow workflow builder in action with headline overlay
2. Pricing comparison — AutoFlow vs Zapier/Make side-by-side (use data from [ALT-338](/ALT/issues/ALT-338))
3. Integration showcase — logo grid of 200+ integrations
4. AI features — visual showing AI-powered error handling and optimization
5. Dashboard/analytics — workflow performance metrics view
6. Free tier callout — "Get started free, no credit card" with feature highlights

**Specs:** 1270x760px, PNG, consistent visual style matching pricing page design from [ALT-338](/ALT/issues/ALT-338).

## Email Headers (Priority 2)

Design reusable email header templates for:
1. **Welcome email** — warm, brand-forward, "Welcome to AutoFlow"
2. **Weekly newsletter** — clean, repeatable header for [ALT-438](/ALT/issues/ALT-438)
3. **Launch announcement** — high-energy, event-style header

**Specs:** 600px wide, optimized for e

title: Pricing Research: Design, Feature & Marketing Ideas for Implementation
description:
## Overview

This ticket compiles all actionable design, feature, and marketing ideas surfaced across the pricing research tickets ([ALT-313](/ALT/issues/ALT-313), [ALT-314](/ALT/issues/ALT-314), [ALT-315](/ALT/issues/ALT-315), [ALT-316](/ALT/issues/ALT-316)). Organized for board review and implementation prioritization.

---

## 1. Website Design & Pricing Page

### Hero Copy (Recommended)
> *"Automation that actually works for you — whether you’re running a business or building one."*
> Subhead: *"No ops team required. Full API access included."*

### Pricing Page Layout
- **Segment toggle** at top of page: "For Teams" / "For Individuals" — surfaces different messaging without cluttering the view
- **4-tier pricing table:** Explore (Free) → Flow → Automate → Scale; Automate visually highlighted as "Most Popular" hero tier
- **Business tier shown first** in table to anchor perception (decoy pricing: Business makes Automate feel affordable)
- **Annual/monthly billing toggle** visible on page load; 30% annual discount shown as monthly equivalent (e.g., "$49/mo → $34/mo billed annually")
- **Beta pricing callout badge** — $5 off all paid tiers during beta ($14/$44/$94)
- **"Most popu

title: Create social media visual templates + ad creative mockups for launch
description:
## Objective
Design visual assets for the upcoming launch campaign. All copy is ready — we need matching visuals.

### 1. Social Media Templates (4)
Design reusable templates for:
- **Twitter/X card** — announcement/feature highlight format
- **LinkedIn post** — professional thought-leadership card
- **Product Hunt gallery** — 5-image set (hero, features, workflow demo, testimonials, CTA)
- **Blog post hero** — reusable template for SEO articles

All templates should follow AutoFlow/Pip brand guidelines.

### 2. Ad Creative Mockups (4)
- **Google Display** — 2 sizes (300x250, 728x90) with automation/AI messaging
- **LinkedIn Sponsored** — 1200x627 image with CTA
- **Retargeting banner** — social proof angle

### 3. Email Header Graphics (2)
- Welcome email header
- Launch announcement header

Use Vercel for prototyping. Save deliverables as issue documents with component code.

**Priority order:** Product Hunt gallery first, then Twitter/X card, then remaining items.

_Replaces [ALT-409](/ALT/issues/ALT-409) which had an unresolvable execution lock._

## Definition of Done
- Figma file with all compon

title: Homepage Hero Redesign — Design mockup
description:
**Parent task:** [ALT-317](/ALT/issues/ALT-317) — Website and App Fixes
**Reference:** [ALT-265](/ALT/issues/ALT-265) — Brand identity (Pip)

## Objective

Redesign the AutoFlow homepage hero section and fix the bottom section. Create a mockup for CMO approval before implementation.

## Specific Requirements (from board)

1. **Hero text centering**: Center the text area next to Pip — "Automate Everything" and all copy in that zone should be centered above Pip
2. **Swipe-in animation**: Hero copy should animate/swipe in when the page loads (smooth entrance, not jarring)
3. **Bottom section cleanup**: The bottom of the homepage is too busy, offset, and overwhelming — redesign it to be clean, balanced, visually organized
4. **Subpages**: We have not framed subpages yet — include in mockup: Features, Pricing, About, Blog/Docs landing pages (at minimum wire-level)

## Input to gather first

- Wait for insights from [ALT-318](/ALT/issues/ALT-318) (Content Lead insights) and [ALT-319](/ALT/issues/ALT-319) (CRO perspective) before finalizing mockup
- Check in with CMO after reviewing those findings

## Deliverables

- Full-fidelity Figma or visual mockup of hero section
- Mockup of cleaned

title: Typography Exploration — Find a fun, expressive font for AutoFlow
description:
**Parent task:** [ALT-317](/ALT/issues/ALT-317) — Website and App Fixes
**Reference:** [ALT-265](/ALT/issues/ALT-265) — Brand identity

## Objective

The current AutoFlow font feels blocky and cookie-cutter. Find (or explore creating) a typography direction that feels more fun, approachable, and unique to the AutoFlow brand.

## Deliverables

### Font Options
- Research and shortlist **3–5 font pairings** (heading + body) that fit AutoFlow brand
- Criteria: fun/expressive, modern, readable, available on Google Fonts or similar (free/open)
- Present each with a sample rendering showing: headline, subhead, body copy
- Consider our brand personality: energetic, quirky, automation-native, not corporate

### Custom Font Feasibility
- Assess whether designing a **custom AutoFlow typeface** is feasible with available tools
- If feasible: create a separate task proposal with scope, approach, and tool recommendations
- If not: note why and which font option best substitutes

## Context

- Brand reference: [ALT-265](/ALT/issues/ALT-265) — Pip mascot, fun/quirky personality, easter eggs
- CMO has final approval on typography direction — present options clearly with pros/cons
- Do NOT implemen

title: Custom Pip SVG Assets & Animations — triggers, website, app
description:
**Parent task:** [ALT-317](/ALT/issues/ALT-317) — Website and App Fixes
**Reference:** [ALT-265](/ALT/issues/ALT-265) — Build our Brand (Pip mascot, brand identity)

## Objective

Replace all stock images and generic icons with custom Pip-themed SVGs across the AutoFlow website and app.

## Deliverables

### Animated Trigger Icon
- **Animated Pip** throwing a lightning bolt for the trigger icon in the app
- Animation plays on hover / initial render, then settles to a static state
- Deliver as SVG with CSS animation or GSAP-compatible animation

### Custom SVG Icon Set
- Replace all remaining stock icons in the app and website with Pip-themed custom SVGs
- Create a small consistent icon set (triggers, actions, workflows, connectors, etc.)
- Match AutoFlow brand: fun, quirky, on-brand with Pip personality

### Brand Logo SVGs for Homepage
- Source actual SVG logos for the brands shown in the homepage integration showcase
- Where official SVGs are available, use those. Where not, create styled placeholder badges.
- **Do not use text-only representations** — use the actual logo marks

## Design Direction

- Reference [ALT-265](/ALT/issues/ALT-265) for brand colors, mascot design, and p

title: Fix design and UX issues on AutoFlow /app page (P1 + P2)
description:
CMO reviewed the live Vercel app at https://autoflow-brand.vercel.app/app and identified the following issues. Please address all P1 items and as many P2 items as possible.

---

## P1 — Critical

**1. Replace emoji icons with SVG icons throughout the app**
The sidebar nav uses emoji and flow nodes use emoji. Replace with proper inline SVG icons consistent with the brand palette (indigo #4A3AFF, teal #00D4B8, coral #FF5F57, yellow #FFD93D). Simple outline or filled icons work well here.

**2. Fix placeholder user in sidebar**
Change "Jane Doe / jane@acme.com" to "Alex Rivera / alex@helloautoflow.com" (or similar professional-sounding demo user).

**3. Make sidebar nav items interactive (or visually indicate they are stubs)**
Currently clicking Analytics/Integrations/Logs/Settings does nothing and there is no visual feedback. Either: (a) add onClick state so clicking changes the active item and shows a "Coming soon" panel, or (b) add a visual indicator they are disabled (reduced opacity, lock icon, tooltip on hover saying "Coming soon").

**4. Fix "All systems go!" and error panels — they should be state-conditional**
- The "All systems go!" panel shows on page load because the cond

title: Fix design polish issues on AutoFlow brand site (P1 + P2)
description:
CMO reviewed the live Vercel site at https://autoflow-brand.vercel.app and identified the following issues to fix. Please address all P1 items and as many P2 items as possible.

---

## P1 — Critical

**1. Fix dead links**
All nav/footer links are `href="#"`. Update:
- Navbar Product/Integrations/Pricing/Docs → anchor to page sections (`#features`, `#integrations`, `#pricing`)
- "See a Demo" → `/app`
- "View Pricing" → add a `#pricing` section or `/app`
- "View all 200+ integrations" → `/app`
- Footer Privacy/Terms/Security → `/app` or `#` is acceptable for now, but they should at least not all obviously be placeholders

Add a social preview image. Options:
- Create a simple 1200x630 PNG or SVG with AutoFlow branding + Pip, save to `/public/og-image.png`

- Also set `twitter: { card: "summary_large_image", images: ["/og-image.png"] }`

**3. Fix product URL in flow demo chrome**
Change `autoflow.app / flows / new-user-onboarding` to `helloautoflow.com / flows / new-user-onboarding`

**4. Add mobile hamburger menu**
The `hidden md:

title: Build brand mockups in Vercel — website and app (AutoFlow / Pip brand)
description:
## Overview

The AutoFlow brand concept has been approved by the board. Your job is to create production-quality mockups deployed to Vercel for both the **website** and the **application/service UI** based on the approved brand document.

**Brand Concept:** [ALT-265 Brand Concept](/ALT/issues/ALT-265#document-brand-concept)
**Parent Task:** [ALT-265](/ALT/issues/ALT-265)

---

## What to Build

### 1. Website Mockup (Vercel)

A Next.js landing page deployed to Vercel with:

**Hero Section:**
- Full-width gradient: `linear-gradient(135deg, #4A3AFF 0%, #00D4B8 50%, #FFD93D 100%)`
- Headline: *"Automate Everything. Flow Anywhere."* in Poppins Bold 700+
- CTA buttons: Primary coral `#FF5F57` = "Start for Free" | Secondary white outline = "See a Demo"
- Pip mascot placeholder (described below) with light trail SVG animations

**Features Section:**
- Flow line SVG motifs connecting workflow nodes
- Integration logo grid (placeholder icons: Slack, Salesforce, HubSpot, GitHub, etc.)
- Dashboard preview with gradient frame + glow

**Footer:**
- AutoFlow logo + tagline
- Social links (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, GitHub, Discord)

### 2. App / Service UI Mockup (Vercel)

A mockup of the core app int

title: Graphic Designer Onboarding: Education, Skills Research & Tools Report
description:
## Mission

You are a Graphic Designer learning to be a Graphic Designer as your primary role in the company.

## Training Steps

### 1. Research Your Role
Go research your job and find a detailed description from a job posting site and resumes for context. Understand what a Graphic Designer does at a tech startup building an AI workflow automation product (AutoFlow).

### 2. Build Your Skills
Use relevant sources and open-sourced books/resources to train yourself around your job description. Create a company skill titled **"Graphic Designer Skills"** and put your training knowledge there. Use the `paperclip-create-plugin` skill or reference the skills workflow to build this.

### 3. Evaluate Current Company Skills
Review the current company skills list and determine which ones would be useful for a Graphic Designer. Document your recommendations.

Currently installed skills relevant to consider:
- `anthropics/skills/frontend-design` - frontend design patterns
- `nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill/ui-ux-pro-max` - UI/UX design intelligence
- `inferen-sh/skills/nano-banana-2` - AI image generation
- `inferen-sh/skills/agent-tools` - 150+ AI apps including image/video generation
-

title: Specify Mission State visual direction, motion, and state behavior for ALT-2099
description:
## Context

Child of [ALT-2099](/ALT/issues/ALT-2099). The Frontend Engineer cannot begin implementation of the new Mission State primary-nav surface until Graphic Designer direction is explicit for the final production surface.

Phase-1 wireframe [ALT-2091](/ALT/issues/ALT-2091) is approved, but the implementation ticket still needs designer-owned production guidance for the new page's unresolved interaction details.

## Ask

Provide the missing designer-owned specification needed to implement [ALT-2099](/ALT/issues/ALT-2099) without visual improvisation:

- Motion/transition direction for page entry and CTA/interactivity states
- Loading, empty, and error-state presentation for each required Mission State card
- Any unresolved iconography, spacing, or surface-token usage details specific to this page
- Explicit signoff that the existing approved wireframe plus these additions are sufficient for frontend implementation

## Constraints

- Reuse the canonical brand tokens already ratified in [ALT-2099](/ALT/issues/ALT-2099)
- Use design-system surface tokens for the approved tonal steps rather than hard-coded new hex values
- Keep scope limited to the Mission Stat

title: Provide visual direction for workflow pop-out builder states
description:
Parent: [ALT-2092](/ALT/issues/ALT-2092).

Frontend implementation is blocked until the workflow pop-out route has designer-owned direction for the new UI states required by the approved spec.

Needed from Graphic Designer:
- Visual treatment for the dedicated full-screen builder entry state when opened from the existing pop-out control
- Explicit read-only state treatment inside the builder route (copy, placement, prominence)
- Explicit workflow-load failure state with a primary "Back to workflows" action
- Any motion/interaction-state guidance for route load, read-only affordance, and failure recovery

Implementation constraints already confirmed:
- The dashboard app already has a canonical builder route at `/builder/:templateId`; reuse is preferred over adding a new route
- The current builder already has a generic error card component, but not a workflow-specific failure state or read-only treatment
- The route is reloadable/shareable through the existing template/workflow id param
- No integration logos are needed for this task
- Source page remains in place; the pop-out is an expansion path, not a replacement

Deliverable:
- Comment on [ALT-2092](/ALT/issues/ALT-2092) with ap

title: Provide social auth visual direction and signoff for dashboard login
description: Frontend implementation for [ALT-1870](/ALT/issues/ALT-1870) requires Graphic Designer direction before UI work begins. Please provide or approve the visual direction for Google/Facebook/Apple login buttons on the dashboard auth surface, including provider logos/icons, layout within the existing auth card, hover/pressed/disabled/error/loading states, and explicit signoff criteria. Existing baseline auth styling may exist in `infra/brand-assets/payload/docs/surface-direction/auth.md`, but this task needs confirmation whether that doc is authoritative plus any missing social-button-specific specs.

title: Logo.Dev
description: Would giving you access to logo.dev help with your ability to provide correct logos for companies?

title: Provide signed auth UI direction for AutoFlow native authentication screens
description:
Frontend implementation for ALT-1740 is blocked pending required visual direction and sign-off from the Graphic Designer.

Please provide a build-ready design spec for the AutoFlow native authentication surface covering:

- Login, signup, and password reset screens
- Typography, colors, spacing, layout, iconography, and responsive behavior
- Motion and interaction states for loading, validation, disabled, error, and success states
- Logo and any auth-specific brand assets needed in the dashboard
- Approval/sign-off on the visual direction before frontend implementation begins

Implementation constraints to design around:

- React dashboard surface, existing app shell
- Must accommodate native auth error states from Microsoft CIAM
- Must work within current Tailwind/Vite frontend stack
- Should avoid introducing motion that materially hurts performance on low-end devices

Please attach/describe the spec in the issue comments so ALT-1740 can proceed once this and ALT-1739 are resolved.

title: Finalize and push high-fidelity marketplace tile refinements
description:
The `autoflow-brand` repository contains 22 marketplace tiles that have been locally refined to high-fidelity (including gradients, descriptions, and verified badges) but are currently unstaged/uncommitted.

Deliverables:

- Merge/rebase with `main` to include latest infra fixes

title: Provide Knowledge Ingest Form visual direction and sign-off
description:
UI implementation for [ALT-1490](/ALT/issues/ALT-1490) is blocked pending Graphic Designer direction. Please provide the required visual spec and approval before frontend build begins.

Needed deliverables:
- Full visual direction for the Knowledge Ingest form in the Data/Knowledge tab
- Typography, spacing, layout behavior, iconography, and asset requirements
- Interaction and motion specs for idle, hover, focus, drag-over, upload-progress, success, error, empty, and disabled states
- Dropzone spec, manual Q&A table spec, validation/error presentation, and responsive behavior
- Explicit sign-off criteria for frontend implementation

Current implementation constraints to design around:
- Existing app uses React + Vite + Tailwind
- Current spec in `research/n8n-tutorial/design-specs.md` is too thin to implement without inventing UI decisions
- Frontend cannot proceed until design direction is approved per AGENTS instructions

title: Provide design direction and sign-off for Linear integration card
description: Frontend implementation of ALT-1391 requires Graphic Designer direction before UI changes. Please provide/confirm: card visuals, spacing/tokens if any overrides, icon/logo asset for Linear in the asset library, and motion/interaction states. Approve final implementation before merge.

title: Design: Provide proposal builder UI spec + sign-off for ALT-907
description: Before frontend implementation on [ALT-907](/ALT/issues/ALT-907), provide approved visual direction and implementation specs: color palette, typography, spacing scale, iconography, interaction states, and motion behavior. Include explicit sign-off criteria for Definition of Done.

title: Routines
description:
Here’s the directive for wiring Graphic Designer outputs into agent routines via MemPalace:



MemPalace + Routines Directive — Brand Context Propagation

Graphic Designer’s outputs need to flow to every agent making visual decisions. Right now that context lives in someone’s head or a one-off handoff. Fix it by making brand artifacts first-class, queryable, and injected into the relevant agent routines.

Canonical brand artifacts to publish and maintain in MemPalace:

Store each as a discrete document with consistent metadata so retrieval is predictable:

	•	brand/competitive-audit — n8n, Make, Kestra, Zapier, Tray, Workato, Pipedream, Activepieces, Temporal/Airflow/Prefect findings

	•	brand/positioning-brief — approved visual positioning + mood direction

	•	brand/design-system — tokens (color, type, spacing, radius, shadow), component specs, iconography rules

	•	brand/motion-spec — easing curves, durations, standard animation patterns

	•	brand/integration-logo-index — manifest of every logo asset (path, variants, license, last updated)

	•	brand/skill-tile-spec — marketplace tile template + populated skill visuals

	•	brand/landing-direction — Next

title: Google Gemini

description:

3. **Testimonial visual treatment** — Design styled avatar/initials treatment for the Social Proof section (3 testimonials)

4. **Blog page header graphic** — A hero/banner graphic for the /blog listing page

## Technical Notes

- Images will be uploaded to Sanity CMS (project `kold

title: Design AutoFlow product UI mockups — dashboard wireframes
description:
Create UI mockups/wireframes for the AutoFlow product dashboard to support launch marketing and product development.

**Deliverables:**
- Main dashboard overview mockup
- Key workflow screens (2-3 core user flows)
- Mobile app version (if applicable)
- Annotated spec for handoff to Frontend Engineer

**Coordination:** Work with the CTO on product requirements and technical constraints. Brand kit from [ALT-643](/ALT/issues/ALT-643) must be applied.

title: Design Google Display and retargeting ad banners
description:
## Objective

Create ad banner set for Google Display Network and retargeting campaigns.

## Required Sizes

- 300x250 (medium rectangle)
- 728x90 (leaderboard)
- 160x600 (wide skyscraper)
- 320x50 (mobile leaderboard)
- 300x600 (half page)

## Ad Variants

Design 3 creative variants per size:
1. **Free tier hook** — "Automate free. No credit card." + CTA
2. **Competitor switch** — "20x more automations than Zapier at lower cost" + CTA
3. **Value prop** — "Put your business on autopilot" + CTA

## Retargeting Variants

2 retargeting-specific creatives per size:
1. **Return visitor** — "Still thinking about it? Start free today."
2. **Trial expiry** — "Your Pro trial ends soon — upgrade to keep full access."

## Specs

- File size < 150KB per banner
- Static images (no animation for initial set)
- Consistent with brand identity
- Clear CTA button on every banner
- Follow Google Ads creative policies

title: Design social media post templates and blog hero graphics
description:
## Objective

Create reusable design templates for ongoing social media and blog content.

## Social Media Templates

Design a template system (not one-offs) for:
1. **Feature announcement** — clean product screenshot + headline overlay
2. **Tip/tutorial** — numbered steps or quick-win format
3. **Stats/metric highlight** — bold number + context
4. **Quote/testimonial** — customer quote card
5. **Comparison** — AutoFlow vs competitor split-frame

**Platform specs:**
- Twitter/X: 1200x675px
- LinkedIn: 1200x627px
- Provide both sizes per template

## Blog Hero Graphics

Design 3-5 reusable blog hero templates that the Content Lead can adapt:
1. Generic "how-to" template
2. Comparison article template
3. Case study template
4. Product update template

**Specs:** 1200x630px, consistent with brand identity. Include editable text areas.

All designs must follow brand guidelines and visual identity from [ALT-338](/ALT/issues/ALT-338) pricing page style.

title: Source and create SVG brand assets for AutoFlow (Pip seasonal variants + automation theme)
description:
Source and create SVG brand assets for AutoFlow. No background/raster images — SVGs only.

**What to produce:**

1. **Pip mascot seasonal/contextual variants** — SVG versions of Pip dressed for different contexts (e.g., launch day, holidays, celebration, working at computer, holding a flowchart). Pip is our friendly robot mascot: rounded body, expressive eyes, teal/indigo color scheme.

2. **Automation-themed illustration SVGs** — icons and spot illustrations relevant to workflow automation (e.g., connected nodes, data flows, gears + circuits, integration connectors, speed lines, checkmarks). These are used on the landing page, docs, and app empty states.

3. **Icon set** — consistent set of 20-30 SVG icons for the app UI (workflow nodes, trigger types, action types, status indicators) matching the brand color tokens.

**Brand tokens (use these for all assets):**
- Primary: Teal `#00D4B8`, Indigo `#4A3AFF`
- Accent: Coral `#FF5F57`, Yellow `#FFD93D`
- Dark: Navy `#0F1333`, Slate `#2D3748`
- Light: Cloud `#F7FAFC`

**Output:** Commit all SVGs to `autoflow-brand/public/brand/` organized by category (`mascot/`, `illustrations/`, `icons/`). Post a summary comment with the file list whe
